findings.title: Tool contracts drift across docs, public schemas, runtime schemas, handlers, and helpers
findings.rootCause: No single generated or tested source of truth for MCP tool input contracts.
findings.impact: Operators can call documented options that do nothing, and dead options can stay exposed without runtime effect.
findings.recommendation: Generate public schemas and docs from runtime schemas or add contract tests that diff every exposed parameter against implementation usage.

findings.title: Bulk governed ingest validates governance metadata but drops it before indexing
findings.rootCause: Governance validation is implemented at the boundary, but indexing/job queue APIs do not carry normalized scope.
findings.impact: Governed bulk import/scan callers can believe metadata is persisted when it is not.
findings.recommendation: Thread normalized governance scope through scan, async ingest jobs, queue processing, and indexMemoryFile.

findings.title: Program metadata drift is systemic across 026, 027, and audit-control packets
findings.rootCause: Generated control artifacts are not freshness-validated against canonical specs, timelines, implementation summaries, and live child inventories.
findings.impact: Resume, release-readiness, and audit synthesis can point at stale or non-executable packet state.
findings.recommendation: Add recursive metadata freshness checks and regenerate graph metadata/description/changelog rollups from canonical packet state.

findings.title: Memory correctness defects split into bounded stale routing and persistent DB/file divergence
findings.rootCause: Cache invalidation coverage and atomic file/DB ordering are incomplete.
findings.impact: Search routing can be stale for up to the TTL after CRUD mutations; save failure paths can leave DB rows ahead of file promotion/finalization.
findings.recommendation: Add entity-density invalidation to shared mutation hooks and make DB commit/file promotion rollback semantics explicit and test-covered.

findings.title: Governance bypasses are real but P0 severity depends on deployment trust boundary
findings.rootCause: Fallback and causal mutation paths bypass or lack scoped ownership checks.
findings.impact: P0 in shared or multi-tenant MCP contexts; P1 in the stated local single-user MCP context.
findings.recommendation: Apply governance filters to fallback member fetches and add scoped ownership checks to causal link/unlink.

findings.title: Fan-out can count non-zero CLI subprocess exits as fulfilled lineages
findings.rootCause: fanout-run returns subprocess exitCode inside fulfilled worker output, while the pool counts failures only when workers reject.
findings.impact: Review/research fan-out orchestration can report success or weak partial success despite failed CLI lineages.
findings.recommendation: Treat non-zero subprocess exit as lineage failure unless salvage proves a complete terminal marker and required artifacts exist.

findings.title: Current CLI fan-out is concurrency-capped, not serial
findings.rootCause: Earlier serial-spawn concern is stale relative to current fanout-pool wiring.
findings.impact: Do not prioritize a serialism fix unless a different non-CLI/native path is under review.
findings.recommendation: Keep concurrency tests; focus remediation on exit-code accounting and iteration-budget propagation.
